title: Process Management
content:
### ps — Process Snapshot

```bash
ps aux                        # all processes (BSD style)
ps -ef                        # all processes (System V style)
ps aux --sort=-%mem           # sorted by memory usage
ps aux | grep nginx           # find specific process
```

### top / htop — Interactive Process Viewer

```bash
top                           # real-time process list
htop                          # improved, color-coded version
```

- `P` — sort by CPU
- `M` — sort by memory
- `k` — kill a process
- `q` — quit

### kill — Terminate Processes

```bash
kill PID                      # send SIGTERM (graceful)
kill -9 PID                   # send SIGKILL (force)
kill -15 PID                  # explicit SIGTERM
kill -l                       # list all signal names
```

### pgrep / pkill — Pattern-Based

```bash
pgrep -u alice                # find PIDs by user
pkill -f "node server"        # kill based on full command line
pgrep -a ssh                  # show PIDs and command names
```

### Jobs — Background & Foreground

```bash
command &                      # run in background
Ctrl+Z                         # suspend current job
jobs                           # list background jobs
bg %1                          # resume job 1 in background
fg %1                          # bring job 1 to foreground
```

### nohup — Ignore Hangup

```bash
nohup long-running-script.sh &
```

### systemctl — systemd Service Manager

```bash
systemctl start nginx
systemctl stop nginx
systemctl enable nginx        # start on boot
systemctl status nginx
systemctl restart nginx
systemctl --type=service --all  # list all services
```
codeExample:
ps aux --sort=-%cpu | head -10
htop
sleep 100 &
pgrep -a python3
systemctl status sshd

quiz.question: What does `systemctl enable nginx` do?
options: Configures nginx to start on boot
